objective: Verify physical existence, condition, ownership, and valuation of inventory; ensure stock records are accurate, complete, and compliant with applicable accounting standards (Ind AS 2 / IAS 2).

steps.action: Obtain inventory count instructions, location list, and warehouse access plan; confirm cut-off date and freeze point procedures.
Define sampling strategy: identify high-value items for 100% count, apply statistical sampling to balance, flag slow-moving or obsolete categories.
Observe physical stock count: independently recount sampled items, record condition, location, and any discrepancies vs. management count.
Perform cut-off testing: review last goods received notes (GRNs) and dispatch records around count date to confirm inventory cut-off.
Assess inventory condition: identify damaged, obsolete, slow-moving, or near-expiry items; obtain management's NRV / write-down estimate.
Reconcile auditor count to stock records (WMS / ERP): investigate variances greater than materiality threshold.
Test inventory valuation: recompute selected items at lower of cost and NRV per Ind AS 2; validate FIFO / WAC / Specific Identification method consistency.
evidence: NRV evidence (selling price - cost to sell)

steps.action: Compile findings, quantify misstatements, issue management letter for stock discrepancies and valuation adjustments > materiality.
Obtain final inventory certificate from management and confirm adjusting journal entries are booked before period close.
Archive count sheets, reconciliations, and working papers with immutable hash anchor.

evidence_checklist.description: Physical count sheets signed by auditor and warehouse team
Cut-off GRN and dispatch records around count date

test_scripts.name: Physical Count Reperformance
test_scripts.sample_rule: 100% of items > ₹5 lakh value; statistical sample for remainder
steps: Arrive at warehouse before management count begins.
Select sample from inventory listing before counts are revealed.
Independently count sampled items and record findings.
Compare to management count — escalate differences > 1% by value.
test_scripts.pass_condition: Auditor count agrees to management count within acceptable tolerance or differences are adjusted.

test_scripts.name: Cut-Off Testing
test_scripts.sample_rule: Last 10 GRNs before and first 10 GRNs after count date; same for dispatches
steps: Obtain last GRN and last dispatch note number before count date.
Verify that all items received up to and including count date are in opening inventory.
Confirm items dispatched before count date are excluded from inventory.
test_scripts.pass_condition: No material cut-off error identified; GIT properly classified.

test_scripts.name: NRV / Valuation Test
test_scripts.sample_rule: All items flagged slow-moving (>180 days) and top 20 items by value
steps: Obtain selling price evidence (recent price lists / invoices post year-end).
Deduct estimated cost to complete and sell from selling price to arrive at NRV.
Compare cost to NRV — confirm lower of cost vs NRV is applied.
Recalculate write-down and agree to financial statements.
test_scripts.pass_condition: Inventory recorded at lower of cost and NRV; write-down is adequate and supportable.

report_sections.title: Scope, Location Coverage And Count Methodology

closure_gates.criteria: Physical count completed and signed off.
Management certificate and adjusting entries obtained.
